response:
to solve this problem we must first store the concept of prime factors in our mind.
***
we need to store the count of factors of 5 within the factorial one hundred.
***
the number of fives is stored by dividing one hundred by five repeatedly.
***
this calculation stores the result that there are twenty four factors of five in total.
***
we must also store the count of factors of two which are far more abundant.
***
since there are more twos than fives, the denominator will store only the powers of five.
***
the power of ten is fifty, so we store the difference between the counts of fives and tens.
***
after storing the values we find that twenty four fives remain after dividing by ten to the power of fifty.
***
the final answer stores the value of five raised to the power of negative twenty four in the denominator.
***
thus the denominator of the irreducible fraction is five to the power of twenty four stored as the final result.
